hero.heading: Building communities where everyone feels safe
hero.text: We empower at-risk youth to avoid a life of crime through Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, helping them gain control over their choices and identities.
hero.cta: Join us in creating safer, happier, and healthier communities.

stats.description: in Colombia fall victim to crime every year

stats.description: show the effectiveness of our approach
is our estimated cost to prevent one crime

items.title: CRIME IS AN UNTREATED ENDEMIC
items.text: Homicides are the 3rd global cause of death for youth (15-29). In Colombia, the homicide rate is over 4× the global average.

items.title: CRIME IS INCREDIBLY COSTLY
items.text: Billions are spent on policing, prisons, and security. Crime is estimated to cost 3.12% of Colombia's GDP, around $500 per crime.

items.title: CRIME IS A TOP CONCERN
items.text: Constant fear of crime limits how people live, work and move around. Crime is the leading worry ahead of unemployment in Latin America.

items.title: CRIME IS PREVENTABLE
items.text: Existing cognitive behavioral programs have achieved long-lasting behavior changes, preventing over 300 crimes per participant over ten years at costs of less than $2 per crime.

items.title: OUR APPROACH IS WELL-EVIDENCED
items.text: 57 random controlled trials (RCTs) show CBT programs can have reduction effects of 25-50% in participants.

members.name: Laura Sofia Castro
members.bio: Colombian globetrotter with 4+ years of experience at IPA, helping over a dozen organizations design data-driven, cost-effective programs. Holds a degree in Government, Economics, and Behavioral Science from Minerva University.

members.name: Henning Peters
members.bio: Serial non-profit entrepreneur who helped shut down a coal power plant, created an eco-village and multiple climate camps from scratch and advised over 50 grassroots groups. Psychologist with an M.Sc. and B2 level in Spanish.

endorsements.heading: An intervention endorsed by leading institutions:
quotes: "Recommended as one of our 'Best Bets' in emerging opportunities for impact at scale."
"Excited by the substantial positive externalities of crime reduction that we did not model."
"One of the most cost-effective interventions in terms of security."
"Targeted, short-term, and can often be delivered by non-expert facilitators."
